questions.q: In which year were the National Film Awards of India first instituted?
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards were instituted in 1954, with the first ceremony held in 1954 honouring films of 1953.

questions.q: The National Film Award for Best Feature Film carries which golden medallion?
questions.explanation: The Best Feature Film at the National Film Awards is honoured with the Swarna Kamal, or Golden Lotus.

questions.q: Who presents the National Film Awards of India?
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards are presented by the Government of India, making them state honours.

questions.q: Across which films are the National Film Awards of India judged?
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards consider films across all Indian languages, making them a pan-India honour.

questions.q: Many National Film Award categories, such as Best Non-Feature Film, carry which silver lotus medallion?
questions.explanation: Many National Film Award categories carry the Rajat Kamal, or Silver Lotus, while the top honour is the Swarna Kamal.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's debut Bengali film, which won the President's Gold Medal for Best Feature Film at the 3rd National Film Awards, was which title?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray's Pather Panchali won the President's Gold Medal for the All India Best Feature Film at the 3rd National Film Awards.

questions.q: The National Film Awards aim to reward cinematic merit free of which kind of bias?
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards aim to reward cinematic merit free of commercial bias, unlike popularity-based awards.

questions.q: How often are the National Film Awards of India presented?
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards are presented annually, honouring films released in the preceding year.

questions.q: Which government body historically organised the National Film Awards for decades before its functions were merged into the NFDC?
questions.explanation: The Directorate of Film Festivals, under the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, organised the National Film Awards for decades.

questions.q: The National Film Awards are widely regarded as which type of honour in Indian cinema?
options: The most prominent film awards in India
A regional award limited to one state
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards are considered the most prominent and prestigious film awards in India.

questions.q: Which Bengali actor won the very first National Film Award for Best Actor in 1968, for his roles in Antony Firingee and Chiriyakhana?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar received the first National Film Award for Best Actor in 1968 for Antony Firingee and Chiriyakhana.

questions.q: The National Film Award for the Best Feature Film on National Integration is named after which film personality?
questions.explanation: The Nargis Dutt Award is given for the Best Feature Film on National Integration at the National Film Awards.

questions.q: Which award, presented alongside the National Film Awards, is India's highest honour for lifetime contribution to cinema?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award, for lifetime contribution to Indian cinema, is announced and presented within the National Film Awards framework.

questions.q: At the National Film Awards, who has traditionally presented the awards to the winners at the ceremony?
questions.explanation: The President of India has traditionally presented the National Film Awards to the winners.

questions.q: At the National Film Awards, the award for Best Female Playback Singer was first introduced in which year, the same year individual acting honours began?
questions.explanation: The National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer was instituted in 1968, alongside other individual achievement categories.

questions.q: The National Film Award for Best Popular Film Providing Wholesome Entertainment carries which lotus medallion?
questions.explanation: The Best Popular Film Providing Wholesome Entertainment is honoured with the Swarna Kamal (Golden Lotus) at the National Film Awards.

questions.q: Which Mehboob Khan film of 1957, an early Indian feature nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film, is a landmark of that era?
questions.explanation: Mother India (1957) was a landmark film and earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: Within the National Film Awards, the Indira Gandhi Award is given for which achievement?
options: Best Debut Film of a Director
questions.explanation: The Indira Gandhi Award honours the Best Debut Film of a Director at the National Film Awards.

questions.q: The National Film Awards include a dedicated set of categories to honour excellence in which non-fiction form?
questions.explanation: The National Film Awards have a full set of categories for Non-Feature Films, covering documentaries and shorts.

questions.q: Director Satyajit Ray, a frequent National Film Award winner, is most associated with the cinema tradition of which city?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray, repeatedly honoured at the National Film Awards, was a towering figure of Bengali cinema based in Kolkata.

questions.q: Which Malayalam director, a multiple National Film Award winner for Best Direction, made his feature debut with Swayamvaram?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan, who debuted with Swayamvaram, won multiple National Film Awards for Best Direction.

questions.q: The National Film Award category for Best Popular Film Providing Wholesome Entertainment was created to recognise which kind of cinema alongside artistic films?
questions.explanation: The Best Popular Film Providing Wholesome Entertainment category acknowledges popular mainstream cinema within the National Film Awards.

questions.q: At the first National Film Awards in 1954, which Marathi film won the President's Gold Medal for the All India Best Feature Film?
questions.explanation: Shyamchi Aai won the President's Gold Medal at the inaugural 1954 National Film Awards.

questions.q: Which actress holds the record for the most National Film Awards for Best Actress, having won the honour five times?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi won the National Film Award for Best Actress five times, a record tally.

questions.q: In which year were separate National Film Awards for individual achievements such as Best Actor, Best Actress and Best Direction first introduced?
questions.explanation: Individual award categories like Best Actor, Best Actress and Best Direction were introduced at the 1968 (15th) National Film Awards.

questions.q: The National Film Award for the Best Film on Environment Conservation or Preservation honours films focused on which theme?
questions.explanation: This National Film Award category specifically recognises films promoting environment conservation and ecological awareness.

questions.q: Which composer, a leading figure of South Indian film music, has won the most National Film Awards for Best Music Direction?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja has won the National Film Award for Best Music Direction multiple times, the most of any composer.

questions.q: The National Film Awards select their winners through which mechanism rather than public voting?
questions.explanation: Winners of the National Film Awards are chosen by an appointed jury of cinema experts, not by public vote or box office.

questions.q: Which K. Viswanath-directed Telugu film, celebrating Carnatic classical music, won the National Film Award for Best Telugu Feature Film at the awards held in 1980?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath's Sankarabharanam won National Film Award honours at the 27th National Film Awards for its celebration of Carnatic classical music.

questions.q: The Marathi film Shwaas, which won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film, was India's official entry to the Oscars in which year's race?
questions.explanation: Shwaas won the Swarna Kamal for Best Feature Film and was India's official Academy Award entry in the 2004 race.
